query: Breva: pick a rhythm, rest a finger on your Mac's trackpad, and follow an orb that expands and contracts complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: OpenAI wants your launches
results.content:
## Press down and breathe

Dvain (@dvain) wanted a reset ritual that didn't mean picking up his phone, so he built Breva: pick a rhythm, rest a finger on your Mac's trackpad, and follow an orb that expands and contracts while the trackpad pulses with every inhale and exhale. Local, no account, no subscription. [...] 🔥 Our Take: Your trackpad has had a haptic engine since 2015, and in ten years its only job has been faking the feel of a click. Breva is the first app we've seen use it for something human: the Mac physically breathes under your finger. Meditation apps live on your phone, the same device serving you the stress. Putting the two-minute reset inside the machine you work on, one keystroke from whatever spiked your heart rate, is the right placement. One commenter said it feels "like the mac itself is breathing with you." That's the whole product, and it's enough.

results.title: ‎Breva: Breathing Focus App - App Store
results.content:
How it works:
1. Choose a breathing technique
2. Pick a session length
3. Rest your fingers on your MacBook trackpad
4. Follow the orb and feel the rhythm through your trackpad
Breva is designed for short, intentional resets during your workday. No clutter, no streak pressure, no complicated setup.
Safety:
Breva is not a medical device and is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any medical condition.
If you have a heart condition, pacemaker, epilepsy, breathing disorder, panic disorder, or any health concern, consult a qualified healthcare professional before using breathing or haptic-based relaxation tools.
Do not use Breva while driving, operating machinery, or doing anything that requires your full attention. [...] Take a calm breathing break on your Mac with visual guidance and trackpad haptics.
Take a calmer breathing break at your Mac.
Breva is a simple Mac breathing app with guided breathing, a soft visual orb, and trackpad haptics to help you slow down, refocus, and reset between tasks.
Built for quick moments when you feel scattered, tense, or overstimulated, Breva turns your MacBook trackpad into a gentle haptic breathing guide.
Features:
- Guided breathing techniques for focus, calm, and stress relief
- Trackpad haptic rhythm designed to keep you present
- Coherent, Box, Calm, and 4-7-8 breathing patterns
- Light and dark modes for a quieter breathing space
- Simple session lengths with recent session history
- Keyboard shortcuts for pausing or ending a session
How it works: [...] # Breva: Breathing Focus

Stress relief with haptics

Only for Mac

Free

results.title: Trackpad on mac book is going crazy! - Apple Community
results.content:
jazzrascal:

This is excessively bad advice! What you are recommending really just amounts to ignoring the symptoms and hoping that the problem goes away. If this is being caused by a swollen battery, ignoring it could result in permanent damage to the computer. If it is actually being caused by someone accessing the computer remotely, ignoring it gives them more opportunities to do something malicious.

Please stop posting this advice everywhere!

Link

nestorb98

Nov 22, 2013 8:30 AM in response to panizbh

My Macbook started doing the very same thing just now. The bottom cover has been deteriorating and is now a twisted piece of unsightly plastic. It has seriously expanded out of the screws that hold it in place. [...] PRAM and SMC resets would only give me a few minutes of respite before a resurgence. If you are going to pay to fix, you need only buy the ribbon cable and chip.

Temporary quick fix: disable trackpad in settings and use a USB mouse.

Attached: The underside of the trackpad with the user side of the computer up, trackpad ribbon cable removed.  
User uploaded file

User uploaded file

Link

Motorcycle Michael

Nov 26, 2013 1:06 PM in response to nestorb98

If you have a white MacBook with a deformed rubberized bottom cover, take it to an Apple store and have it replaced (free):

Linc's advice (above) for trackpad issues is about as comphresive as it gets. Moisture, hand lotions, and built-up static electricity are all common causes of erratic trackpad operation and easily solved.

Link [...] disable and enable the trackpad. Until Apple solves this problem I will not purchase, or recommend anyone else

purchase, a MacBook of any kind.

Link

boxlike

Nov 19, 2013 2:11 PM in response to panizbh

That little sticker (pictured) was sitting flush against the center bus on the trackpad chip. If a little drop of liquid gets in along the top of the trackpad, it can travel down the ribbon and to sticker. Add a little heat from the adjacent battery and its ink can be deposited onto the bus.

As I'm sure you've now guessed, this is what happened to me. It seemed to be causing an interference along the top of the trackpad [visible in the 'Show Live View' feature of Better Touch Tool (Freeware)].

results.title: Fix MacBook trackpad not working in 2026 – Official steps
results.content:
Close menu icon
Setapp logo

# MacBook Pro trackpad not working? Here’s how to fix it

Olha Novitska
card-background-image

CleanMyMac

### Fix common MacBook issues — faster with the right tools

CleanMyMac repairs disk permissions, clears junk, and runs maintenance tasks that fix many stubborn Mac problems. Available on Setapp.

shield
`com.apple.preference.trackpad.plist`
`com.apple.AppleMultitouchTrackpad.plist`

Trackpad not working on your Mac? It's frustrating, particularly when you can't bring a spare mouse along.

Yet, fixing a failed Mac trackpad can be simple, depending on its cause. Sometimes, resetting your Mac can make the trackpad operational again. But often, you'll need to try other methods before you can start moving the mouse pointer again. [...] Also, watch for battery swelling – on many MacBook models (especially 2020–2023 M-series units that sat unused with low charge for long stretches), a swollen battery is still the most common hardware reason for a "dead" or stuck trackpad click. Gently press around the edges of your MacBook's bottom case or check if it rocks on a flat surface. The battery sits right under the trackpad, and swelling pushes upward, making clicks unresponsive or gestures erratic.

If you spot any bulging, stop using the Mac immediately, unplug it, and get it to an Apple Store or authorized service provider ASAP, or contact Apple Support for guidance Swollen batteries are a safety risk and usually covered under service programs if within limits.

## How to fix the trackpad on your MacBook [...] On an Apple chip-powered Mac, follow these steps.

If your trackpad has failed because of hardware issues, the diagnostic results will reflect that. Jot down the reference code so you can communicate the issue when you send your Mac for repair.

Apple Diagnostic works well in identifying hardware failure, but what if your trackpad is infected by malware?

Remove them with CleanMyMac's built-in protection tool, which helps keep your machine safe by finding and removing harmful files. CleanMyMac can also boost Mac performance by removing unnecessary files.

Here's how.

Wait for the scan to complete. Then, review the results and delete junk files.

results.title: How to use MacBook trackpad: Mac trackpad gestures you should know | Macworld
results.content:
If you can remember the following 10 gestures, you’ll take steps toward being more efficient with your trackpad use while reducing strain on your hands. Even if it takes a moment to remember them all, there’s one additional benefit beyond saving time by using gestures. There should also be a reduction in the frequency that an annoying and task-disrupting gesture is used accidentally on the trackpad.

Once you have mastered these gestures take a look at these 8 secret Mac trackpad gestures you won’t believe you didn’t know.
